ocr_texts: S Seattle @New England Spread Unit 1.0 Seattle -4.5-110 +1689 59-38-2in Last99 NFL ATS PicksW +191 3-1-1in Last5SEAATS Picks Analysis: We don't have room to lost all of the reasons we prefer Seattle, but we'll try. Consider the odd dynamics in the AFC this season, wherein many contenders struggled and saw QBs get hurt, and schedule dynamics which allseemed to breakin the Patriots'direction. This unique confluence of events helped get New England here but across the line is a different story. Recent Super Bowl underdog success (five straight covers) can be minimized a bit as recent "Supe" spreads have been so thin (1, 2, and 1 point the past three three),almost pick'em situations. Bottom line? The Seahawks have enough offense and definitely enough defense to avenge thatgrating Super Bowl xLixloss eleven years ago. Play Seahawks

text: Micah Roberts
ocr_texts: Feb082026,6:30pmEST Seattle -@NewEngland Sacks DeMarcus Lawrence Unit 1.0 Over 0.25 Total Sacks -109 Analysis: Drake Maye led all quarterbacks, taking 62 sackson the year,regularseason and postseason combined.And now we've got a sack-hungry Seattle defense that is starving to get a taste of Maye. The guy who has my immediate attention is DeMarcus Lawrence. The Seahawks haveplayed playoff teams in their last five games,and Lawrence had a sackin three of his last four starts. I'm feelingpretty good about Lawrence getting at least a half-sackwith Maye as the starting quarterback.

Feb 08 2026,6:30pm EST Seattle @ New England Rushing Yards Rashid Shaheed 0ver 2.5 Unit 1.0 Total Rushing Yards -118 Analysis: Seattle wantsto be conservative but also showsome newmoveswithsomenew runners,including Rashid Shaheed. In their last game against the Rams, they tried one run, and he got O yards, but in his first playoff game,he had two attempts for 27 yards.Zach Charbonnet played that game, and he is out with an injury.They can only run Kenneth Walker so much before running out of options, and using the dynamite explosiveness of Shaheed could be the secret weapon. That's what makes him so valuable. Use him. Get him involved in the gameplan. He's a game changer. He had 9 carries for 69 yards in the regular season and 3 for 27yards in the two postseason games.

Feb 08 2026,6:30 pm EST Seattle @New England Rush + Rec Yards Kenneth Walker Ill Over Unit 1.0 98.5 Total Rushing + Receiving Yards -115 Analysis: I'm waiting on a Super Bowl prediction,but I thinkI know where I'm going, and I think1 know where Kenneth Walker is going. Without Zach Charbonnet, now, Walker is going to carry the ball at least 20 times in this game, get over 125 yards rushing and receiving combined,and at least one TD scored, which sets me up for quite a few props. The Seahawks have won 9 straight games,and in the last three,Walker has gotten over 111 yards in all three.Walker has been on it in four of his last five games, and the last five gameswere all playoff teams. Walkerover98.5 rushing and receivingyards.

082026,6:30pmESl Seattle -@NewEngland Spread Unit 1.0 Seattle-4.5 -110 +523 22-15in Last 37 NFL Picks +181 12-9 in Last 21 NFL ATS Picks +658 11-4 in Last15 NE ATS Picks Analysis: The Seahawks and Patriots were the top teams in each conference,with each only losing three games, and each covering 14 games on the season. The Patriots went 9-0 in away games, and the Seahawks went 8-1 in road games. Seattle won nine games in a row, with the last five games being against playoff teams and covering the last four. Drake Maye has looked shaky over the last few weeks, as has his offensive line. The Seahawks were No.1in the NFL against the rush number, 6th in total yards allowed (293.4), and No.1in total points (17.1) allowed. The real betting hasn't begun. I think this line moves through5 and 5.5 quickly and closes 6.

text: RJ White
ocr_texts: Feb 08 2026, 6:30 pm EST Seattle @ NewEngland 1st Half Spread 1st Half New England +3.5 unit 1.0 -120 +46 4-3 in Last7NFL Game Props Picks Analysis: The Patriots aren't being given much of a chance in this game, but I see their defense as being good enough to limit Seattle's scoring and give Drake Maye a chance at the win. I expect scoring to be tough in the first half after the long lead-up to kickoff,with nerves at an all-time high in the first quarter. I believe there's only three points of difference between these teams on a neutral field, and while I wait to see the best number I can get on the Pats in the full game, l can't pass up the opportunity to grab the hook on the first-half line. Pick Made: Feb 04, 8:17 am EST on FanDuel

Feb 08 2026, 6:30 pm EST Seattle @ New England 1st Half Total Unit 1.0 1st Half Under 22.5 -110 +46 4-3in Last7 NFL Game Props Picks Analysis: I expect this to be a slow-starting game for both offenses as they feel out two elite defenses, so much so that one prop from my 60 picks article is on the first touchdown to be scored in the second quarter. In that type of environment, I don't see three TDs being scored by these teams in the first half, so I'm willing to take this total all theway down to 20.5 and get plus odds on the Under. Pick Made: Feb 04, 8:19 am EST on BetMGM

Seattle @NewEngland Carries Unit 1.0 Sam Darnold 0ver 2.5 Total Carries +112 +394 33-26 in Last 59 NFL Player Pr0ps Picks Analysis: We know that Drake Maye is going to use his legs to try and pick up first downs when needed, but no one is really talking about the other QB in the matchup. Darnold has had 3+ rush attempts in six of his last seven games, and with the Patriots getting creative at sending pressure during the playoffs, 1 believe it's likelyhe'll be on the move a couple times in this game, and he'll certainly not hesitate to try and pick up a first down if he sees open field.We could also hit this by virtue of end-game kneeldowns if the Seahawks do what's expected and win this game. I like getting plus odds value on this prop. Pick Made: Feb 05,2:08 pm EST on FanDuel

Seattle @ New England Home Team Total New England Under 20.5 Unit 1.0 Total Pts-115 +297 4-1in Last5 NFL Team PropsPicks Analysis: The Patriots have endured a monster run of defenses to get to the Super Bowl,and waiting for them is the league's No. 1 scoring defense. The last opponent to get to 300 yards against Seattle besides the Rams was the Cardinals in Week 10, and the Seahawks have allowed two totaltouchdownsin six games against non-Rams opponents since Week 12. Drake Maye had one game with a sub 60% completion rate heading into the playoffs and has been below that number in all three games.The Seahawks should excel against the Patriots' offensive line and continue to keep Maye uncomfortable,and Sam Darnold is playing well enough to not expect the Pats offense to get short fields.l love getting this above 20.

Seattle @ New England Receiving Yards Mack Hollins 0ver 25.5 Unit 1.0 Total Receiving Yards -110 +394 33-26 in Last 59 NFL Player Pr0ps Picks Analysis: This is obviously the biggest game of the year for both teams, and big games are where Hollins shows up the most. Much has been made about the Patriots' easy schedule, but when they played the Buccaneers,Ravens and in the second game against the Bills, Hollins recorded his three performances with at least eight targets this year. He also had seven against a tough Browns defense. Hollins returned from the IL in the AFC Championship to catch two passes for 51 yards in a game where Drake Maye threw for just 86 yards total. Hollins has outperformed Stefon Diggs in several key games this year and could do so again here.

Hollins has outperformed Stefon Diggs in several key games this year and could do so again here.

text: Brady Kannon
ocr_texts: Feb082026,6:30pm EST Seattle @ New England Money Line Unit 1.0 Seattle -208 +1208 29-15-2 in Last 46 NFL Picks W Analysis: Key to playing the game this way is waiting Betting the dog on the ML is very common when it comes to Super Bowls. By the time we get to game day it is likely that the ML on the favorite will be less than -200, so be patient and see if you can get apreferred price. I would not be surprised if Seattle covered the spread as well. I personally made the number 5with 1point included for "Home Field Advantage." The Seahawks have played here for 14-seasons, they and their fans are much more familiar with this venue. My stats crunch comes to 6.5-but again, l will play Seattle ML as I believe, simply, they are the better team. Pick Made: Jan 29,11:28 am EST on BetRivers

Feb 08 2026,6:30pm EST Seattle @ New England Anytime TD Scorer Unit 1.0 AJ Barner Anytime TouchdownScorer+240 Analysis: Barner has gotten very little attention in the 2playoff gamesfor the Seahawkssofar, catching just 3 balls for 15 yards. 1 believe this setsup nicely forhim to come in under the radar somewhat for this game. We have to figure that JSN will be the focal point of the New England defense, limiting his output as much as possible. This could create opportunitiesfor Barner,who caught 6 touchdown passes this season and also scored a rushing TD.With the opponent's defense dedicating its attention so heavily on Smith-Njigba and Kenneth Walker, I like Barner to have a successful game and find his way into the end zone. Pick Made:Feb 01,3:51pm EST on DraftKings

Seattle @ NewEngland Interceptions Drake Maye Over 0.5 Unit 1.0 Total Passing Interceptions-147 Analysis: Maye has not been his MvP self in the postseason. Conditions have been difficult but so have been the defenses he'sfaced and it won't get any easier vs Seattle. In 3 games,he is 43 of 77 with 6 fumbles (3 lost), 2 interceptions, and he's been sacked 15 times. Even if his shoulder is healthy, I still feel he deserves to be a solid favorite to throw an interception. The O-Line hasn't been able toprotect him aswell and he has faced the best pass defenses in the league. Chargers No. 1, Houston 2, Denver 4, and Seattle No. 5 as far as Defensive Passer Rating rankings in the NFL. I'm betting he will slip at least once again on Sunday. Pick Made:Feb 01,3:41 pm EST on DraftKings

text: **Empire Algorithm Picks** 🔥
ocr_texts: sacks with 12 total, while Seattle has just 3 across their two games. The Patriots also have 5 that's still a massive edge in defensive disruption. When you combine the historical trends, this year's playoff performance, and the betting splits, this sets up as a near-perfect spotfor thePatriots. As for the first half under, this sets up just as well. In the last six Super Bowls, we have consistently seen these games start slow,with four of the last six staying under 23 points in the first half. This year, that trend makes even more sense when you look at the matchup. Both teams are extremely run-heavy and naturally conservative in their approach, and on top of that,both head coaches are making their Super Bowl debut. That matters. In games like this,especially early,coaches tighten up,play thefield position game,and prioritize not making mistakes over taking risks. I fully expect a very cautious start, with limited fourth-down attempts, plenty of punts,and both teams taking the three points whenever they're available instead of forcing anything. This is the type of Super Bowl where the first halfis more about calming the nerves and honestly,it would not surprise me at allifwe don't even see more than one touchdown scored in the entire first half. Everything about this game script points to a slow, controlled, and conservative opening, which makes the first half under a very strong play.

33,42,25,31,24,28,38 A big reason for that is Maye's ability to scramble, and Iexpect that to be on full display tonight. I'd be more than happy to take his rushing yards over because not only will they have designed runs for him,but he will also look to scramble when Seattle blitzes and tries to pressure him. During the regular season,he ran 103 times for 450 yards and 4 touchdowns,and inthe playoffs he's already at 24carries for 141yards and 1 touchdown.This will bea real problem forSeattle. If Seattle uses their normal approach of stacking the box and bailing out against the run, the play-actiongame willbewide open and allow thePatriots tohit those short and intermediate throws to the outside and middle of the field, which is exactly what Maye does best. If Seattle chooses to play two-high and not stack the box, the Patriots are going to run right through them. Either way, this Patriots offense is set up to move the ball and score, especially with Maye coming off games against elite pass rushes like Denver and Houston. On the other side,Iexpect Darnold to come back down to earth tonight against one of the most underrated defenses in football,Quietly,this Patriots defense ranks: #2 in opponent points per game #2 in opponent yards per game #4 in points per play #4 in opponent rushing yards per game #2 in opponent rushing touchdowns per game Darnold has looked great in the playoffs, throwing for 470 yards, 4 touchdowns, and 0 interceptions,but here's the key:Darnold has not gone more than 3 games all season without throwing a pick or having a bad game. Look at what happened earlier this year: Came into the game vs the Rams with 2 straight games without a pick and threw 4 interceptions. Came into the game vs Pittsburgh with 2 straight games without a pick and threw 2 interceptions. He's thrown 14 interceptions this season, and I fully expect that to continue tonight, especially against a defense that thrives on pressure and forcing mistakes. I also expect which playsright into thePatriots strengths. He's also going to have to look away from Smith-Njigba tonight with Christian Gonzalez likely shadowing him. While the Patriots do play a lot of zone, Iexpect them to mix in more man coverage to allow for more blitzing and more pressure on Darnold, which is exactly when he starts to make mistakes and take sacks.The Patriots lead the playoffs in

Empire AlgoPicks,Saturday February 7th 2026 (2U) Patriots+4.5(-105) (1u)Patriots/SeahwaksFHUNDER23(-130) Handicapping the Super Bowl is always easier than most games because of the sheer volume of money that gets bet on this one night alone. This year we're looking at roughly S1.8 billion wagered onjust this game, and if you think sportsbooks are going to lose on the most bet game in the history of sports, especially after already losing the most money they game where the books position themselves to win big The betting splits tell the story clearly. The public is all over Seattle, sitting at 65% on the spread and 65%on the moneylinewhilehaving the same for the over,which sets up a classic spot where the public wantsSeattle and the over and the sportsbooks want NewEngland andtheunder. Now let'stalk about how theSuperBowlis actuallyhandicapped. This game is all about historical trends, no matter how you slice it, going back decades, the same patterns show up again and again, and this year they line up almost perfectly for the Patriots. Here are the key trends that matter: In the last 22 Super Bowls,when the underdog(Patriots)played their previous game under the total and the Super Bowl total is set under 55, the underdog is a perfect 10-0 ATS. When one team plays their conference championship game away and the other plays it at home, the team that played away is 10-1 ATS in the Super Bowl over the last 24years. Theworse playoff seed (Patriots are 2,Seahawks are 1)is 17-2 ATS in the last 29 years. The underdog is 18-6 ATS in the last 24 Super Bowls and 14-4 ATS in the last 18Super Bowls. All ofthese trends go back to around the year 20o0,which is when the Super Bowl truly became a massive betting event.Back in 1991,this game had only S50-75 million wagered on it. Now we're talking billions every year. The NFL and the sportsbooks understand this market extremely well, and historically, this is the exact type of spot where the value ends up on the underdog, which is the Patriots tonight. The narrative is that this Seattle defense is so good that it's going to completely shut down Maye and the Patriots' run game, but that couldn't be further from the truth. This Patriots team has gone through the toughest defensive schedule in the playoffs this season, facing the Chargers, Texans, and Broncos, and they beat all three. Yes, they didn't score a ton of points, but all of those games were played in freezing cold conditions. When the Patriots have played in warm weather this season (over 55 degrees),they've scored:

Here are the key trends that matter: In the last 22 Super Bowls,when the underdog(Patriots)played their previous game under the total and the Super Bowl total is set under 55, the underdog is a perfect 10-0 ATS. When one team plays their conference championship game away and the other plays it at home, the team that played away is 10-1 ATS in the Super Bowl over the last 24years. Theworse playoff seed (Patriots are 2,Seahawks are 1)is 17-2 ATS in the last 29 years. The underdog is 18-6 ATS in the last 24 Super Bowls and 14-4 ATS in the last 18Super Bowls. All ofthese trends go back to around the year 20o0,which is when the Super Bowl truly became a massive betting event.Back in 1991,this game had only S50-75 million wagered on it. Now we're talking billions every year. The NFL and the sportsbooks understand this market extremely well, and historically, this is the exact type of spot where the value ends up on the underdog, which is the Patriots tonight. The narrative is that this Seattle defense is so good that it's going to completely shut down Maye and the Patriots' run game, but that couldn't be further from the truth. This Patriots team has gone through the toughest defensive schedule in the playoffs this season, facing the Chargers, Texans, and Broncos, and they beat all three. Yes, they didn't score a ton of points, but all of those games were played in freezing cold conditions. When the Patriots have played in warm weather this season (over 55 degrees),they've scored:

NCAAB PLAYS (3U)MICHIGAN-10.5-110 Michigan looks a class above Ohio State heading into this matchup. The Wolverines have been one of the nation's most dominant teams all season,ranking near the top nationally and riding a strong winning streak. Their size and efficiency insideshould overwhelm an OhioStateteam thatstruggles to defend the paint, making it hard for the Buckeyes to keep pace. Even on the road, Michigan is the better team by a wide margin, and I expect the Wolverines to cover the 10.5- point spread. George Holani has failed to exceed 8.5 longest reception in 8 of his last 9 games (1.4 longest reception/game average). RHAMONDRE STEVENSON(NE)O75.5RUSH+RECYARDS -110 Rhamondre Stevenson has exceeded 75.5 rush + recyds in 7 of his last 8 games (96.2 rush + rec yds/game average).

cashitbaby ALLPLAYS2/8/2026 NFLPLAYS (5U) SEAHAWKS-4.5-110 New England made it to Super Bowl LX, but the path 一 and the performance-leave plenty of questions:The Patriots struggled offensively throughout the AFC title game, benefited from weather and opponent mistakes, and faced one of the softest schedules in football along the way. Seattle,meanwhile,looks battle-tested and complete. Mike Macdonald's defense has thrived in high-leverage moments, and Sam Darnold is playing the best football of his career. With superior balance, experience, and momentum, this matchup strongly favors the Seahawks to control the game and pull away on the biggest stage. NBA PLAYS (4U) CELTICS-4 -110 After a brutal blowout loss to Detroit, New York is in a tough bounce-back spot heading into Boston. The Knicks have struggled on the road all season and haven't fared wellat TD Garden, while the Celtics are rolling on a five-game win streak built on elite defense. With Boston playing consistently well even without Jayson Tatum —and New York dealing with multiple injury concerns —the matchup favors the Celtics, especially if the 3-point shooting shows up early. I'll side with Boston to get it done at home.
